title: Mount ConfigMap as Volume
description:
## Inject Configuration via a ConfigMap Volume

An application reads its configuration from files on disk at `/etc/app-config/`.

**Your tasks:**
1. Create a **ConfigMap** named `app-config` in the `default` namespace with the following key-value pairs:
   - `environment`: `production`
   - `log_level`: `warn`
   - `max_connections`: `200`
2. Create a **Pod** named `config-reader` using image `busybox:1.36`
3. Mount the ConfigMap as a **volume** at `/etc/app-config` inside the container
4. The pod should run the command: `sleep 3600`

hints.title: Write the Pod manifest
hints.body: Use a heredoc piped directly to kubectl apply -f - to avoid writing to a temp file.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: config-reader
spec:
  containers:
  - name: reader
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
    volumeMounts:
    - name: config-vol
      mountPath: /etc/app-config
  volumes:
  - name: config-vol
    configMap:
      name: app-config
EOF

hints.title: Verify
hints.body: Confirm that the ConfigMap files are visible inside the container.

validation.description: Verifies ConfigMap exists with correct keys and the pod mounts it at the right path.
